title: Authentication Failure Redirect Codes for Analytics Tracking
story: As a product administrator, I want authentication-related redirects from app.datatoolspro.com to datatoolspro.com to include a redirect code parameter, so that I can track failure reasons in PostHog analytics and display contextual help messages to users.
description:
When users are redirected from `app.datatoolspro.com` to `datatoolspro.com` due to authentication issues, the redirect should include a `redirectcode` parameter identifying the specific failure scenario.

### Goals
1. Capture every scenario where the app redirects users back to the marketing site
2. Assign a unique redirect code per scenario
3. Display contextual messages to users based on the code
4. Track redirect frequency per code in PostHog analytics
5. Prevent frustrated users from signing up multiple times

### Approach
First, catalog all redirect scenarios in the authentication flow and assign codes. Then implement parameter passing and dynamic message display on the datatoolspro.com /beta page.
acceptance_criteria: Every authentication redirect from app to marketing site includes a redirectcode parameter
Each redirect scenario has a unique code
Marketing site displays contextual messages based on the redirect code
PostHog can track redirect codes for analytics
Repeated sign-up attempts by the same user are flagged or prevented
ux_changes: Contextual error/help messages displayed on the marketing site based on redirect code. Users see relevant guidance instead of a generic page.
middleware_changes: All authentication redirect paths updated to include redirectcode parameter. Catalog of redirect scenarios and codes.
implementation_notes: Waqar raised questions about how to generate redirect codes for all scenarios, particularly post-login redirects. IP-based tracking is unreliable due to dynamic IPs. Need to define the full list of redirect scenarios and their corresponding codes.
comment_summary: Waqar asked for clarification on redirect code logic, noting that signup clicks are straightforward but other redirect scenarios (failed login, successful login redirects) are unclear. IP-based identification was considered but rejected as unreliable.
